caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the concept of Figure of Merit (FOM). The presenter, Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, explains how to calculate FOM using given values. The slide shows two equations: \(-\frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f} = 2772.1\) and \(-\frac{W_l}{\dot{m}_f} = 476.0\). These values are used to compute the FOM, which is defined as the ratio of \(\frac{W_l}{\dot{m}_f}\) to \(\frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_f}\). The calculation results in an FOM value of 0.1717. Additionally, there is a thermodynamic diagram on the right side of the slide, illustrating various processes such as constant temperature (T=const), constant pressure (p=const), and constant enthalpy (h=const) paths, which likely relate to the cryogenic processes being discussed. The video aims to provide a clear understanding of how to evaluate the efficiency of cryogenic systems using FOM.
